meaning: vt.使...气馁,使...沮丧,使...失去勇气
examples: Don't let this defeat dishearten you.
Don t let this defeat dishearten you.
Bad credit can dishearten you in many ways.
Mr Djankov worries that the indiscriminate sell-off of recent weeks may dishearten reformers.
The miss did not dishearten them though and with 65 minutes gone they got a deserved but controversial equaliser.
